meta.description: Meta-analysis of SOTM trends across 2023-2025 — technology, design, interaction, sound, performance, mobile

rendering.name: Three.js
rendering.verdict: Safe, respected choice. Won't lose points but won't gain 'innovative framework' points either.

rendering.name: React Three Fiber (R3F)
rendering.verdict: Valid for portfolio sites, but SOTY winners tend to use vanilla Three.js for maximum control.

rendering.name: Custom WebGL / WebGPU engines
rendering.verdict: Signals the highest technical capability. Developer Award territory.

uiFrameworks.name: Svelte / SvelteKit
uiFrameworks.trend: Strong upward — Igloo SOTY used it
uiFrameworks.verdict: Currently the 'smart' choice for creative developers. Signals technical awareness.

uiFrameworks.name: Next.js (React)
uiFrameworks.status: Still dominant overall but declining for creative sites
uiFrameworks.marketShare: 40-50% of all award winners, declining for creative/immersive
uiFrameworks.trend: Stable for e-commerce/portfolio, declining for immersive
uiFrameworks.verdict: Won't lose points but increasingly seen as 'default' rather than 'chosen'.

uiFrameworks.name: Nuxt.js (Vue)
uiFrameworks.verdict: Valid choice, especially for French agencies.

uiFrameworks.name: Astro
uiFrameworks.trend: Rising for portfolio and content sites
uiFrameworks.verdict: Excellent for content + WebGL islands approach.

animationLibraries.name: GSAP 3 + ScrollTrigger
animationLibraries.verdict: Expected. Not using GSAP is a statement.

animationLibraries.name: Framer Motion
animationLibraries.verdict: Good for React, but GSAP is more flexible for WebGL integration.

animationLibraries.name: Custom animation systems
animationLibraries.verdict: Building custom animation = Developer Award signal.

scrollLibraries.name: Locomotive Scroll v5
scrollLibraries.verdict: Using v5 early signals awareness and Locomotive connection.

scrollLibraries.name: Custom scroll physics
scrollLibraries.verdict: Event Horizon opportunity: gravitational scroll physics.

emergingTech.opportunity: Compute shaders, better particle systems, potential AI integration
emergingTech.risk: Browser support gaps require WebGL 2.0 fallback
emergingTech.recommendation: Use WebGPU with WebGL 2.0 fallback. The WebGPU path is the 'wow' for cutting-edge judges.

emergingTech.readiness: Vision Pro, Quest — still niche for web
emergingTech.opportunity: Differentiator if jury members have spatial devices
emergingTech.risk: Most jury evaluates on desktop/mobile — VR is bonus, not core
emergingTech.recommendation: Nice-to-have, not critical for Event Horizon.

emergingTech.risk: Limited relevance for Event Horizon's use case
emergingTech.recommendation: Not relevant unless streaming live telescope data.

in.name: Deep space
in.description: Near-black backgrounds with subtle blue/purple undertones

in.name: Warm monochrome
in.description: Cream/beige with black text — editorial, elegant

in.name: Neon accent on dark
in.description: Dark base with single vibrant accent color

in.name: Atmospheric gradient
in.colors: Dynamic gradients that shift based on scroll/interaction
in.description: No flat colors — everything is gradient and atmosphere

colorPalettes.eventHorizonFit: Deep space palette is perfect. Black hole = deep black void, accretion disk = warm orange/red/white gradient, gravitational lensing = blue-shifted light on approach side. The color palette IS the astrophysics.

in: Typography as hero element (not just support)
Sans-serif dominance for headlines, serif for body (reversal from 2022)
out: Ultra-thin weights for body text (readability issues)
Google Fonts defaults (Inter, Roboto — too common)
typographyTrends.eventHorizonRecommendation: Use a custom or premium typeface. Monospace for scientific data (coordinates, measurements). Variable font for the main headline — weight animation as gravity increases.

in: Minimal navigation chrome — content IS the navigation
Horizontal scroll sections within vertical flow
Scroll-driven animations via ScrollTimeline API (native browser)
Horizontal scroll sections with snap points
out: Scroll-triggered reveal-only animations (entrance animations without substance)
scrollInteraction.evolving: Jury becoming more accepting of native scroll behavior — hijacking must be justified by the experience. Offering an 'accessible mode' with native scroll is increasingly valued.

in: Custom cursor that reflects site personality
Cursor as physics object (mass, velocity, collision)
cursorInteraction.eventHorizonIdea: Cursor as gravitational source — particles near cursor deflect. Near the black hole, cursor itself is pulled toward the singularity (magnetic attraction to the center). Cursor trail becomes spaghettified near event horizon.

soundTrends.status: Increasingly expected for immersive experiences. 4 out of 5 recent SOTY winners had sound.
in: Spatial audio (Web Audio API with panning)
Ambient soundscape that evolves with user progress
Muted by default with elegant enable prompt
Audio reactive to visual state (louder when more happening visually)
out: Sound that doesn't match the visual mood
bestPractices: Always mute by default — respect user agency
Elegant sound enable prompt (not a browser default dialog)
Sound enhances, never competes with visual
Provide easy mute toggle throughout experience
Consider reduced-motion users — sound-only experience option?

layers.sound: Near-silence with ultra-subtle high-frequency hiss (cosmic microwave background)
layers.implementation: White noise generator with extreme high-pass filter, very low volume

layers.sound: Deep, low-frequency oscillation — like a heartbeat of spacetime
layers.implementation: Oscillator at 20-40Hz with slow LFO modulation, amplitude tied to visual proximity

layers.sound: Swirling, granular texture — heated matter orbiting
layers.implementation: Granular synthesis with spatial panning following disk rotation

layers.sound: Subtle pings and resonances as particles orbit and collide
layers.implementation: Procedural: each particle collision triggers a short sine burst, pitch based on velocity

layers.sound: All previous sounds pitch-shifted downward, time-stretched, increasingly distorted
layers.implementation: Real-time pitch shifting via Web Audio API playbackRate, convolution reverb increasing

layers.sound: Overwhelming bass drop → sudden silence → inverted audio (reversed sounds)
layers.implementation: Compressor with extreme ratio, then gain to zero, then reversed buffer playback

layers.sound: Inverted, alien audio landscape — familiar sounds playing backwards, information paradox as audio
layers.implementation: Reverse all audio buffers, add heavy convolution reverb, introduce Hawking radiation as high-freq digital noise

evolution.2020: 60fps on desktop was impressive. Mobile could be 30fps.
evolution.2024: 60fps on desktop mandatory. Mobile 30fps minimum, 60fps preferred. Adaptive quality expected.
evolution.2025: 60fps everywhere or adaptive system that maintains perceived smoothness. Performance monitoring visible to judges.

desktop.loadTime: Under 3 seconds to first meaningful paint

mobile.loadTime: Under 5 seconds to first meaningful paint
mobile.adaptiveQuality: Mandatory — mobile GPU variance is extreme

lowEndDevices.expectation: Graceful degradation — beautiful static or minimal animation fallback

performanceExpectations.performanceAsJudgingCriteria: Jury members test on their own devices. If it's choppy on a 2-year-old MacBook Pro, it will score lower. Mid-range hardware is the real target, not high-end gaming PCs.

evolution.2018: Mobile was optional for WebGL sites
evolution.2020: Mobile expected but could be simplified significantly
evolution.2022: Mobile must maintain the essence of the desktop experience
evolution.2024: Mobile is evaluated as seriously as desktop. ~40% of jury tests on mobile.
evolution.2025: Mobile-first thinking even for desktop-primary experiences. The mobile experience must stand on its own.

currentBestPractices: Design mobile experience independently, not as a responsive squish
Touch interaction model designed specifically (not just 'hover → tap')
Performance budget: mobile gets its own quality tier
Native scroll on mobile (smooth scroll libraries often worse than native on mobile)
Orientation awareness — portrait vs landscape can offer different views
Reduced particle counts, simplified shaders, lower resolution textures on mobile
Battery-aware: reduce animation intensity when battery is low
Network-aware: detect connection speed and adjust asset quality

approach: Simplified black hole: mesh-based sphere with custom shader instead of raymarching (much cheaper)
Touch interaction: tilt device to orbit, scroll to approach
Native scroll with scroll-triggered animations (no hijacking)
Reduced post-processing: no bloom, simplified grain
Prefers-reduced-motion: static image with scroll-reveal content
Portrait orientation optimized (95%+ of mobile users in portrait)

definitelyIn: Custom GLSL shaders with visible artistic purpose
riskZone: Full scroll hijacking (acceptable if justified, risky if not)
Full WebGL UI (impressive but accessibility concerns)
Very long experiences (>5 minutes — jury attention span)
